title: Chronic cough review

examination.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
examination.rationale: Supports chronic-cough history and examination for chronic cough review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

investigations.text: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.
investigations.rationale: Supports basic investigation and referral context for chronic cough review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

follow_up.text: Defines chronic cough in adults as cough lasting more than 8 weeks and sets the statement scope.
follow_up.rationale: Supports adult chronic-cough definition and scope for chronic cough review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines chronic cough in adults as cough lasting more than 8 weeks and sets the statement scope.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Interval course, pattern, triggers, sputum, haemoptysis, exposures, medicines, associated symptoms, examination, investigations, diagnosis and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for chronic cough review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
